main_sections.title: Waiting for the Rain
main_sections.prose_summary: Waiting for the Rain by Kamakshi Balasubramanian is a quietly philosophical narrative set in an unnamed village during a severe drought. Velu, a hard-working farmer who has cultivated his small piece of land for six years without a break, opens the story by scanning a cloudless sky, muttering that the day does not look encouraging. The text immediately establishes both his character — disciplined, rational, resistant to superstition — and his crisis: the rains have failed to come for months, the fields lie cracked and bare, and the farmers wait helplessly. When a neighbour suggests consulting astrologers, Velu firmly rejects the idea and instead makes a practical journey to the city weather office, only to be told by bemused forecasters that they themselves cannot explain the anomaly. Returning home tired and thirsty, Velu rests under a large tree and encounters an old woman, whose wrinkled skin and sparkling eyes suggest she is both aged and wise. She interprets his bitterness as an invitation to counsel him. At first she gently puzzles Velu by suggesting he may have worked 'too hard'; when he protests, she redirects his attention not to himself but to the land. The earth, she explains in a soft, smiling voice, is ancient — it has worked for thousands of years through every ploughing, sowing, and planting season. Just as human beings need rest, the land too needs a fallow pause, and when rain does not fall, nature is providing exactly that respite. The land lies undisturbed, resting and replenishing itself; and when the rains finally arrive, it will spring back to fertility with renewed vigour. Velu walks home slowly, turning this idea over in his mind, moving from skepticism toward an emerging peace. As he approaches the village in the evening, a cool breeze touches his back, a single drop lands on his shoulder, and clouds gather in the distance. Rain is coming. Velu runs home laughing. The story is an understated ecological parable about soil health, the wisdom of natural cycles, and the importance of listening to traditional knowledge, framed through the encounter between a rational modern farmer and an ancient voice of the earth.

reading_for_comprehension.section_name: Let us discuss + Let us think and reflect

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Complete the table given below. One example has been done for you. Share your answers with your classmates and teacher.

question_bank.stem: What was said: 'It doesn't look encouraging.' — Who said: Velu — to himself — He looked up at the sky. (example done)
question_bank.table:
What was Said|Who Said|What was Said|Who Said
It doesn't look encouraging.|Velu|to himself|He looked up at the sky.
The rains came all these years without any such consultations…|Velu||
So many favourable conditions but still no rain.|||

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Do you think the old woman would help Velu? If yes, why? If no, why not?
Will Velu's wait continue or will it rain?
Complete the following statements given below with suitable reasons.

question_bank.stem: Velu asked the old woman to stop smiling because _________________.
Velu said that it was hard not being able to work since _________________.
Velu was confused when the old lady said that the land needed rest because _________________.
The old lady said that it was good for the land when it didn't rain as _________________.
Velu ran home laughing and happy because _________________.

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
Read the given extracts and answer the questions that follow.

Extract 1: "...I can't see how talking to astrologers will bring rain." He decided to go to the weather office in the city and talk to someone. But the people at the weather office said they couldn't really tell him when the sky would gather clouds and bring rain. "We are at a loss ourselves!" they exclaimed. "So many favourable conditions but still no rain. Very odd!"

question_bank.stem: What does the first line of the extract tell us about Velu's beliefs?

Complete the following sentence with a suitable reason. Velu is determined to seek information about the rain because ___________________. (he was at a loss of words / he wants to find a practical solution to the drought affecting his farm)
Complete the table with reference to Velu's weather office visit: Expectation (What I think will happen) | Outcome (What actually happened)
question_bank.table:
Expectation (What I think will happen)|Outcome (What actually happened)
||

question_bank.stem: Complete the following sentence with a suitable option. The absence of rain despite favourable conditions, shows ___________________. A. lack of astrological knowledge B. ineffectiveness of farmers like Velu C. delay in understanding the weather D. unpredictability of nature

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
Read the given extract and answer the questions that follow.

Extract 2: "The soil, the land, the earth... shouldn't someone let the land rest a bit?" the old woman said, softly, smiling. "Rest? Let the land rest? I don't understand," Velu looked at her, a little puzzled. "Yes, my son, that is the difficulty. You don't realise that the earth is old..."

question_bank.stem: The old woman's advice was accompanied with a soft tone and a smile. What does that tell us about her?
Fill in the blank with the correct option from those given below. The old woman's advice ___________ Velu, at that moment. A. failed to impress B. confused C. scared D. made sense to
State one thing about the old woman when she referred to Velu as 'my son', while offering advice.
Complete the following sentence with a suitable explanation. The 'difficulty' that the old woman mentions is due to the fact that ________________.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Answer the following questions.

question_bank.stem: How does the old woman convey Nature's wisdom to Velu?
The writer says, 'The fields lay untended…' Why couldn't the farmers tend to their fields in the absence of rain?
Support the old woman's perspective that the land needs rest just like people do.
What can be inferred about Velu's character based on his reaction to the drought?
What does the story suggest about the life of farmers and their relationship with nature?
How might the rest and care of land contribute to a balanced and sustainable ecosystem?

listening.transcript_text: Hello and welcome to today's weather forecast. Delhi is expected to have a generally cloudy sky with light rain. The temperature will range between a minimum of 25 °C and a maximum of 36 °C. The sky will generally be cloudy with moderate rain in Mumbai. The minimum temperature is 24 °C and the maximum will be around 30 °C. Moving eastwards, Kolkata will have a partly cloudy sky with one or two spells of rain or thundershowers. The minimum temperature will be 28 °C and the maximum will be around 34 °C. Chennai is expected to have a partly cloudy sky with a possibility of thunderstorm. The minimum temperature will be 27 °C and the maximum will be around 36 °C. Bengaluru will have a generally cloudy sky with light rain. The minimum temperature will be 21 °C and will rise to a maximum of around 29 °C. Hyderabad is expected to have a generally cloudy sky with light rain or drizzle. Temperature will be between a minimum of 23 °C and a maximum of 33 °C. That's all from me today. Have a beautiful day ahead. Don't forget to carry your umbrellas!

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Listen to the weather forecast. As you listen, fill in the blanks with the exact word you listen to.

question_bank.stem: Delhi will mostly have a ___________ sky with light rain.
Moderate rain is likely to happen in ___________.
Kolkata is likely to have a maximum temperature of around ___________ °C.
There is a chance of a ___________ in Chennai.
Bengaluru will have a ___________ temperature of 29 °C.
Taking an ___________ is advisable because of the weather conditions.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Here is a tongue twister about 'weather'. Practice saying it as fast as you can. 'Whether the weather be fine, or whether the weather be not, whether the weather be cold, or whether the weather be hot, we'll weather the weather, whatever the weather, whether we like it or not.'

Rain has arrived after a long dry spell on the Earth. Imagine the Rain and the Earth have a conversation. Work in pairs and present the role-play. You may start: Rain: Greetings, Earth! It's been a while since we've met. How have you been? Earth: Hello Rain! I am so glad to see you. Continue using the provided hints for Rain and Earth.

question_bank.stem: Perform a role-play in pairs: Rain and the Earth have a conversation after a long drought, using the given hints for each character.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: You have observed the difficulties faced by the people in your neighbourhood due to scarcity of water especially during the summer months. Write a letter to the Councillor of your Municipal ward, requesting her/him to set up a rain water harvesting unit. Provide suggestions to address the issue. Remember to: use formal language, keep sentences short and to the point, state the purpose clearly and concisely, give relevant details. Format: Sender's address, Date, The Municipal Councillor (designation and address), Subject, Dear Madam/Sir, Body (4 paragraphs: Drawing attention to problem / Causes and Consequences / Request and Suggestions / Conclusion), Yours sincerely, Signature.

question_bank.stem: Write a formal letter to the Municipal Councillor requesting installation of a rainwater harvesting unit in your ward.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Solve a crossword using picture and word clues. All clues are related to the words from the text that are connected to weather or agriculture. Down: 1. gentle wind; 3. rumbling sound with rain; 4. grains etc. grown in large quantity in fields. Across: 5. collected crops; 8. (sun image); 9. prepare and use land for crops; 10. turn up the field soil; 11. plant seed/s.

question_bank.stem: Solve the crossword using the given picture and word clues related to weather and agriculture.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: The text uses words like 'piece,' 'weather,' 'son,' and 'rain'. These words are the same as other words that are spelt differently—peace, whether, sun, and reign. Words that are pronounced the same as other words but differ in meaning or in spelling are called homophones. Now, complete the paragraph by filling in the correct option of homophones given in the brackets.

question_bank.stem: Fill in blank 1: The farmer looked at the sky and wondered ________________ (weather/whether) the clouds would finally bring rain.
Fill in blank 2: He sat down on the ground, breaking a small ________________ (piece/peace) of roti for his meal.
Fill in blank 3: The ________________ (plain/plane) fields stretched endlessly before him, completely dry.
Fill in blank 4: He thought about how he could ________________ (sell/cell) his last sack of grain.
Fill in blank 5: Without the rains, it didn't ________________ (seam/seem) possible to grow another crop this season.
Fill in blank 6: every farmer hopes for the next ________________ (rain/reign) by nature to bring life back to the land.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Choose the correct meanings from the given options for the underlined words or phrases in the following sentences.

question_bank.stem: Being annoyed, she muttered something which nobody heard. (i) spoke in a low voice (ii) spoke slowly (iii) spoke angrily
At the new work place, Riya felt forlorn for a week. (i) upset and irritated (ii) angry and bitter (iii) sad and neglected
When all his friends had gone, he was at a loss because he did not know what to do. (i) confused (ii) shocked (iii) lonely
When the little child saw her father, her eyes sparkled. (i) glowed dimly (ii) showed joy (iii) opened wide

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Match each word (adjective) in Column 1 with a word (noun) that collocates in Column 2. Write the adjective-noun collocation in Column 3. One example has been done for you: favourable — country — favourable weather.

question_bank.stem: Match adjectives with collocating nouns: wrinkled, dejected, fragrant, ceaseless, beloved with: activity, weather, skin, garden, expression.
question_bank.table:
Adjective|Noun Options|Collocation
favourable|(i) country|favourable weather (example)
wrinkled|(ii) activity|
dejected|(iii) weather|
fragrant|(iv) skin|
ceaseless|(v) garden|
beloved|(vi) expression|

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Underline the verbs and identify the forms of tenses for the following sentences from the text: 1. Oh, it is hard not to be able to work... 2. …I am too old. 3. What are you smiling at, Amma? 4. But my son, that is what I am talking about. 5. The earth has worked for years, centuries… 6. I have worked hard and honestly. Forms: Simple Present Tense (1 and 2), Present Progressive Tense (3 and 4), Present Perfect Tense (5 and 6).

question_bank.stem: Identify the tense forms in the given sentences.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Now, fill in the blanks with the correct form of Simple Present, Present Progressive or Present Perfect tenses of the verbs given in brackets. Ravi (i) __________ (be) a farmer who (ii) __________ (practise) crop rotation on his land. Each season, he (iii) __________ (plant) different crops to maintain the soil's health. This year, he (iv) __________ (grow) legumes, which help restore the nutrients in the soil. Over the years, Ravi (v) __________ (learn) that planting the same crop repeatedly (vi) __________ (deplete) the soil, making it less fertile. By rotating crops, he (vii) __________ (keep) the land productive and fertile. He (viii) __________ (see) a noticeable improvement in his yields since adopting this method. Now, Ravi (ix) __________ (share) his knowledge with other farmers, explaining how crop rotation (x) __________ (benefit) both the soil and their harvests.

question_bank.stem: Fill in blank (i): Ravi __________ (be) a farmer
Fill in blank (ii): who __________ (practise) crop rotation
Fill in blank (iii): he __________ (plant) different crops
Fill in blank (iv): he __________ (grow) legumes
Fill in blank (v): Ravi __________ (learn)
Fill in blank (vi): planting the same crop __________ (deplete) the soil
Fill in blank (vii): he __________ (keep) the land productive
Fill in blank (viii): He __________ (see) a noticeable improvement
Fill in blank (ix): Ravi __________ (share) his knowledge
Fill in blank (x): how crop rotation __________ (benefit) both the soil and their harvests

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Study the highlighted determiners in sentences from the text. Learn the use of common determiners: Articles (a, an, the), Demonstrative (this, that, these, those), Possessive (my, our, your, his, her, its, their, one's), Definite Numeral (one, two, three…), Indefinite Numeral (some, any, no, all, much, many, few, less, several, little), Distributive (each, every, either, neither). Now, fill in the blanks by choosing the correct determiner from those given in the brackets.

question_bank.stem: Fill in blank 1: It was ________ (a/an) rainy afternoon
Fill in blank 2: ________ (a/the) sky was thick with dark clouds
Fill in blank 3: in front of ________ (her/their) house
Fill in blank 4: loved playing in ________ (a/the) rain
Fill in blank 5: jumping from ________ (some/one) puddle to another
Fill in blank 6: spotted ________ (few/a few) snails
Fill in blank 7: With ________ (each/every) jump
Fill in blank 8: both ________ (her/their) squeals and laughter
Fill in blank 9: ________ (either/neither) siblings minded it

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Rest is very important for us to function in a proper manner. Even the Earth needs rest. This is known as fallowing. Read the facts given below related to fallowing: A fallow year refers to leaving the land without sowing for one, or several vegetative cycles. This is done to allow the land to recover its nutrients and retain moisture. It also helps to avoid spreading of diseases and controlling pests. The sustainable land management method has been applied for centuries, wherein a field is divided into two halves—one is planted with crops while the other remains unplanted. Then, the process is reversed in the following year.
Find out from your Vocational Education teacher about Agriculture as a vocation and other related vocations, such as horticulture, etc.

question_bank.stem: Find out about Agriculture and related vocations (horticulture, etc.) from your Vocational Education teacher.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: India's rich tradition of meteorological knowledge includes indigenous forecasting systems, grounded in ancient wisdom. References to rain and weather are found in numerous ancient texts, including the Rigveda, Shrimad Bhagavad, Parashara Samhita, Panini's Ashtadhyayi, and Meghmala. These texts offer insights into rainfall patterns and seasonal changes. Meteorological Services for Agriculture in India: The primary objective of the Agricultural Meteorology Division, India Meteorological Department (IMD) is to minimise the impact of adverse weather on crops. For detailed information, visit: https://mausam.imd.gov.in/responsive/servicesMetEnvironment.php
